cases.explanation_text: Application app-88231 was declined. The loan-to-value of 87% [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march] exceeds the policy maximum of 80% [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march] after the 35% collateral haircut [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march]. The credit score of 612 is above the referral floor, so the decline is policy-driven, not score-driven.
cases.decision_facts: Recorded outcome: decline. Re-derived LTV 87%, policy max 80%, haircut 35%, score 612, referral floor 600.
sources: [credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march] Collateral haircut policy (March 2026): 35% haircut, 80% max LTV.

cases.explanation_text: Application app-88231 was declined mainly because the applicant's employment history was weak and the property was in a declining postcode. The loan-to-value was also somewhat high.
cases.decision_facts: Recorded outcome: decline. Re-derived LTV 87%, policy max 80%, haircut 35%, score 612, referral floor 600.
sources: [credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march] Collateral haircut policy (March 2026): 35% haircut, 80% max LTV.

cases.explanation_text: So, regarding application app-88231, there are a number of factors that came into play here, and while it can be complex, ultimately what happened, after considering many things, is that the loan-to-value of 87% [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march], which as you know is computed after the haircut of 35% [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march], came out above the 80% [cite:credit-policy:CP-001|v2-march] threshold, and the score of 612 was fine.
